title: Can't resolve internet hostname correctly
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Inside the container `nslookup` resolve an external FQDN but exits with code 1 and outputs `** server can't find podman.io: NXDOMAIN`.
The result is that I can't connect to internet services.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run a container with alpine image and exec a shell
```bash
podman run --rm -it alpine sh
```
2. inside the container make a DNS query
```bash
nslookup podman.io
```
3. verify the exit code
```bash
echo $?
```
4. try a http call
```bash
wget https://podman.io/
```

**Describe the results you received:**

For the DNS lookup i get the IPs but program exits with error (note the error on NXDOMAIN)
```
Server:         192.168.1.1
Address:        192.168.1.1:53

** server can't find podman.io: NXDOMAIN

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   podman.io
Address: 185.199.109.153
Name:   podman.io
Address: 185.199.108.153
Name:   podman.io
Address: 185.199.110.153
Name:   podman.io
Address: 185.199.111.153
```

The exit code the above nslooup is "1", so there is an error somewhere, but the FQDN is resolved.

From `wget` invocation I get:
```
wget: bad address 'podman.io'
``` 

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect that `nslookup` exits with code "0" and without the error output `** server can't find podman.io: NXDOMAIN`.
And that `wget` calls the external server.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The host works without errors.
Call other containers within a container works (I use docker-compose and aardvark-dns)
Inside the container I'm able to call external services by IP, for example:
```bash
wget https://185.199.109.153 --header='host: podman.io' --no-check-certificate
```
So I deduce that is not a network/firewall issue, at least not on TCP/80/443 connections.
And I tried to call the same DNS server from the host via nslookup, the result is ~~without error~~ the same.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.3
Git Commit:   814b7b003cc630bf6ab188274706c383f9fb9915-dirty
Built:        Sun Nov 20 23:32:45 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
So is `192.168.1.1` in your example the aardvark-dns ip or is it your upstream dns server?
Do you run rootless or as root? How does /etc/resolv.conf look on your host and in the container?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since the question was never answered, I am going to close this issue.  Reopen the issue, if you still want us to work on it.
